brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies all three as MST instances with valid formal mapping and cycle-removal proof; goes three levels deep — surface mapping, formal mathematical reduction, and input-density subtlety; catches the complete-vs-sparse distinction as a potential trap but does not explicitly name reasoning biases; verifies that Problem B's 'no redundant road' constraint is automatically satisfied by MST optimality, but no counterfactual or alternate-angle re-derivation; acknowledges the density difference but dismisses it without structured confidence quantification; never seriously entertains the possibility of non-isomorphism or an alternative framing; every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation step reproducible by a third party; explicitly avoids the surface-similarity trap by drilling to the formal G=(V,E,w) definition and verifying structural identity; zero evidence of ad-hoc reasoning or unverified structural similarity claims; follows topology almost exactly — enumerates components via table, pairwise-compares, identifies deepest shared property, validates universality, extracts unified whole, checks scope boundary, and verifies.
